title: Daily Briefing — Apr 28
headline: Tech rips, but sector rotation is subtle — flows keep us honest.
body: Right, so the market continues to grind higher, led by the usual suspects — SPY at $715.17 is moving toward the 20 MA ($685.04), with breadth improving as mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL) all rocket well above their short-term moving averages. VIX is normal, volatility contracting, and Fear & Greed is in classic greed territory at 68 — not extreme, but more than enough to warrant caution. If it's in the press, it's in the price.

Sector rotation shows XLK (Tech) and XLB (Materials) firmly leading, while XLF (Financials) and XLY (Discretionary) are improving with elevated risk. Staples (XLP) and Communication (XLC) lag, signalling ongoing risk-off sentiment underneath the headline tech run. Watch Energy (XLE), now weakening — don't blindly chase the previous leaders.

Contrarian signals building: after last month's extreme Fear spike (now reversed to Greed), we're due for mean reversion. No major rule triggers on bonds (HYG, LQD) — both holding above 20 MA, so risk-on still stands. But tech's vertical move (AAPL, NVDA, ARM, DELL) and retail FOMO means abundance mindset: don't chase highs, bank gains, and wait for weekly 20 MA pullbacks. If Smart Money starts to dry up near highs, reduce exposure. Less trades = more wins.
callout_body:
SPY: $715.17 — Approaching 20 MA ($685.04).
NVDA: $216.61 — Watch for consolidation above $190.84 (SMA20).
XLF: $51.81 — Improving, but SMA200 ($52.29) is resistance.
XLK: $160.57 — Leading, 20 MA ($146.30) is mean reversion.
AAPL: $267.61 — Approaching SMA20 ($262.34).
HYG: $80.51 — Above all MAs, no bond breakdown.
Fear & Greed: 67.9 — Greed, but not extreme.
ENERGY: XLE $56.77 — Weakening, below SMA20 ($57.61).
action_items: Trim tech longs as price stretches above short-term MAs, look for mean reversion setups near weekly 20 MA
Watch Financials (XLF) — improving but not out of the woods. Hold, but stay defensive until SMA200 ($52.29) is cleared
closing_quote: Don't chase — if you missed the rocket, catch the next train. Abundance mindset.
